contents:
# Obangame Express 2026 exercise concludes 15th iteration in Cameroon

## Overview
The 15th iteration of Exercise Obangame Express closed in Cameroon, targeting piracy, illegal fishing, trafficking, and maritime attacks. It stressed the need for responses matching Gulf of Guinea security challenges.

## Details
Commanders highlighted ongoing threats like armed robberies and illicit activities in the exercise wrap-up. Multinational units participated in scenarios mimicking actual incidents. UKMTO coordination with Gulf of Guinea services remained noted in broader context. No casualties or specific vessels involved, as it represented a concluding training phase. The event reinforced regional commitments to counter piracy surges.[3][4]

**Location**: Cameroon, Gulf of Guinea

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @MDAT_GoG, which covers Gulf of Guinea piracy maritime security armed attacks. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following Gulf of Guinea piracy maritime security armed attacks will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
